fact: April 19 is exactly 109 days into the year, which means there are exactly 256 days left - and 256 is a perfect power of 2!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for cherry blossoms to explode into full bloom, creating pink snow when the petals fall!
The sun usually rises around 6:20 AM and sets around 7:30 PM in many mid-latitude places, giving you over 13 hours of daylight to play outside!
April 19 falls in the middle of Aries season, when the ram constellation rules the sky!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves typically turn brilliant orange and red before falling!
Migrating hummingbirds are often zooming through North America right now, beating their tiny wings up to 80 times per second as they race north!
The soil temperature often hits around 50°F in many northern regions, which is when earthworms become super active and start wiggling everywhere!
April 19 sits exactly 29 days after the spring equinox, meaning daylight is growing by about 2-3 minutes every single day in northern places!
Dandelions are typically popping up like crazy right now in northern climates, and each fluffy seed head contains about 150-200 seeds ready to fly away on the wind!
In many temperate regions, this is when morning dew gets super heavy because warm days meet cool nights, creating perfect conditions for grass to get soaking wet!
Tadpoles in ponds are often starting to sprout their back legs right about now in northern areas, getting ready to hop out of the water as tiny frogs in a few weeks!
